intro: Anathallis iguapensis, also known as the Iguape Anathallis, is a species of orchid native to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. These compounds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antiviral, and anticancer activities, making the plant a subject of interest in traditional medicine. The Iguape Anathallis is traditionally used to prepare teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
